input.headline: Heimir Hallgrímsson assesses World Cup play-off draw

raw.headline: Heimir Hallgrímsson assesses World Cup play-off draw

winner: Heimir Hallgrímsson walked up to a vending machine, selected 'World Cup tickets,' and said, 'I hope I don't draw a blank this time.'

candidates.text: Why did Heimir Hallgrímsson bring a ruler to the World Cup play-off draw? Because he heard they were doing yard measurements instead of goal-line technology!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is specific to the context, uses a play on 'yard measurements' versus 'goal-line technology,' and includes a reference to Heimir Hallgrímsson. It's somewhat generic in its setup but still clever and concise."}

candidates.text: Heimir Hallgrímsson just said that he's excited to draw against the World Cup... a board game.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, surprising, and specific, playing on the double meaning of 'draw' (football match vs. board game). It's slightly undermined by being a bit predictable in its wordplay."}

candidates.text: Heimir Hallgrímsson, after drawing the World Cup play-off: "I've been a coach for years, but this time I feel like I should've studied geometry instead!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, specific to the context of a World Cup play-off draw, and uses a surprising twist by connecting coaching experience with geometry, which adds humor through unexpected relevance."}

candidates.text: Why did Heimir bring a map to the World Cup play-off draw? Because he wanted to mark his territory!
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 6.5, "reason": "The joke is somewhat generic and relies on a common pun ('mark his territory'), which may not be surprising or specific enough to fully engage the audience."}

candidates.text: Heimir Hallgrímsson walked up to a vending machine, selected 'World Cup tickets,' and said, 'I hope I don't draw a blank this time.'
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 7.5, "reason": "The joke is concise, uses a specific reference to the World Cup play-off draw, and includes a pun on 'draw a blank,' which adds surprise and humor. However, it could be more directly tied to Heimir Hallgrímsson's role or the actual event for greater specificity."}
